title: Got several errors after updating local libpod to commit id 1a93857a
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

1. [podman] \<defunct\>
2. ERRO[0000] error joining network namespace for container

```shell
[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ git rev-parse HEAD
1a93857acc4ee1e5a9213e2c22f12802d84cd277

[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ ./bin/podman system service --timeout 0 tcp:127.0.0.1:8080 &
[1] 76135

[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ ps axuf|grep -iE '[P|p]odman'
fedora     76135  0.0  0.8 1493332 32924 pts/0   Sl   08:31   0:00  |           \_ ./bin/podman system service --timeout 0 tcp:127.0.0.1:8080
fedora     76144  0.0  0.8 1272216 33736 pts/0   Sl   08:31   0:00  |           |   \_ ./bin/podman system service --timeout 0 tcp:127.0.0.1:8080
fedora     76147  0.0  0.0      0     0 ?        Zs   08:31   0:00  |           |       \_ [podman] <defunct>
fedora     76148  0.0  0.0  44112  1276 ?        S    08:31   0:00 ./bin/podman

[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ ./bin/podman ps
ERRO[0000] error joining network namespace for container 6e9e319d44db0d73de693f758fdcfda2ccb328b27b7393417eab1e11cfe6025a: error retrieving network namespace at /run/user/1000/netns/cni-68934d3c-a27e-2e9c-9388-f68ec0f4c7ac: unknown FS magic on "/run/user/1000/netns/cni-68934d3c-a27e-2e9c-9388-f68ec0f4c7ac": 1021994
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE   COMMAND  CREATED  STATUS  PORTS   NAMES

[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ ls /run/user/1000/netns/ -lah
total 0
drwxr-xr-x. 2 fedora fedora  80 Jul  7 08:20 .
drwx------. 8 fedora fedora 180 Jun 16 02:53 ..
-rw-r--r--. 1 fedora fedora   0 Jun 29 13:36 cni-68934d3c-a27e-2e9c-9388-f68ec0f4c7ac
-rw-rw-r--. 1 fedora fedora   0 Jul  7 08:20 cni-a20c63ac-f8e8-6071-a551-6562d57a1877
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. as Description

**Describe the results you received:**
as Description

**Describe the results you expected:**

```

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Openstack VM instance

comments.body:
Thanks for the report, @chuanchang!

The error comes from containernetworking/plugins and the statfs type is a tmpfs (see below).

```C
#define TMPFS_MAGIC		0x01021994
```

CNI is expecting PROCFS_MAGIC, or NSFS_MAGIC. @giuseppe, do you have a suspicion how we could end up with a tmpfs one?

it seems just that the mount was not pinned so it gets the stat from the underlying `/run/user/1000` mount.  It should be:

```
$ podman unshare stat -f /run/user/1000/netns/cni-6ea426db-d093-3b88-f41e-4f61a57ea74a 
  File: "/run/user/1000/netns/cni-6ea426db-d093-3b88-f41e-4f61a57ea74a"
    ID: 0        Namelen: 255     Type: nsfs
```
if the podman pause container died the mount namespace wasn't kept alive so we lost access to these pinned namespaces.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
